TargetTimeDataView.description:
Indicates which ITime the callers should prefer when reporting a singular ITime.

 - REQUESTED: Prefer the requested_integration_time when reporting a singular ITime.
 - ACTUAL: Prefer the actual_integration_time when reporting a singular ITime.

chargeModel.title: Used for calculating cost for this pattern

type.title: For dithering patterns type is always target

TimeAccountingDataTimeAccountingStatus.description:
Lifecycle states time accounting entities transition through.

 - NOT_STARTED: No exposures configured by the entity have been observed.
 - STARTED: A subset of exposures configured by the entity have been observed.

An `ExposureEntity` will never be in this state. Technically an `ExposureEntity`
is started after `sgo` has been sent, but before the exposure is complete.
However, we currently don't create `ExposureEntity`s in Kealahou until after observation.
 - OBSERVED: All exposures configured by the entity have been observed, but at least one is not validated.
 - VALIDATED: All exposures configured by the entity have been validated.

userToken.title:
this is the PI of the program that this entity will be recorded for
if null should be derived using the program_data.contact_info.user_token

identifier.description:
Valid identifier in the catalog.

Note: this ID will contain a catalog name, because spacing matters in catalogs, the full ID for an object is like
M    4. Catalog is M, id is identifier could be 4, but to reference m4, you need the spaces.

NB: These identity values can not be set in fits headers because they do not have to conform to
the length restrictions.

associatedObservingBlocks.description:
select ob.* from ob, prg, icseq where ob.prg_id = prg.id
and prg.runid=<runid>
and icseq.ob_id=ob.id and ob.con_id = <constraint.sybase_cons_id>
and icseq.ic_id = <instrument_configuration.sybase_ic_id>;
associatedObservingBlocks.title:
When just creating an OT, this list is empty
When this OT belongs to an OG, then any OBs in sybase that this OT is related too must be filled in.
select * from blah where (select id from prg where prg
Query to find related OBs is, select ob.* from ob, prg where ob.prg_id = prg.id and prg.runid=?     instrument_configuration.sybase_ic_id, constraint.sybase_cons_id

The maximum number of OBs which can be omitted but still provide science value.
It's main purpose is to assist the scheduling/weighting algorythm.

Used to sample a target at a given frequency.

It is a very simple pattern which means, observe every night if the target is visible.

SingleObservationGroup means one observing block. It's the simplest observation structure, and has no contingencies
on any other observations.

the total snr achieved of all exposures
 of all observing components of this snr block

carryForwardSnr.title:
if the program is copied to a new program possibly for a new semester
 the cumulative snr will be moved to the carry_forward_snr and the
 carry forward snr will be counted in the cumulative snr of the new program

totalExposures.title: counting the total validated exposures for this OC (OT/TARGET)

persistenceState.title:
- OBSERVED: For most algorithms, we treat observed and validated the same.  Observed just indicates that
there is a possibility it could fail validation

token.description:
Unique identifier for the target.

When creating a new target, this will be inferred from the request data
if left blank here.

Must begin with the program token followed by a dash, e.g.
20AE01-0123456789

name.description:
Name for the target entered by PI, or primary name as resolved by CDS.

Will become OBJECT in the FITS header.

Required.
Length limited to 39 characters.
Allowed characters are letters, numbers, spaces, or any of:
!@#$%^&*()_-+.,?/[]<>

label.description:
Index of the target within the program, set by the system automatically.
Used for reference to targets in communications with QSO staff.

persistenceTargetDataMagnitude.description:
Estimated/measured magnitude values per-band.
Each value is optional, except where noted.

ephemerisPoint.description:
The ephemerides that define the path of motion for the target.

Must be provided using topocentric astrometric coordinates.
Do not provide apparent coordinates.
CFHT strongly recommends the use of JPL Horizons System for
generating the proper coordinates. CFHT's observatory code is T14.

At least five distinct positions must be provided that span the
desired observing period. The first position should be well in
advance of the observation. The positions do not need to be
uniformly sampled throughout the desired observing period.

The points will be interpolated to determine the position of the
target at the time of observation, at which point there must be
at least one ephemeris point defined in the past, and at least
four defined in the future.

Requested radial precision for the measurement in meter per second.

If the value is absent, then accurate radial velocity measurement was not requested
If the  value is present, then we need to take extra care with guiding to ensure we
can meet the requested accuracy

When absent, the PI never confirmed a linking for this target. The coordinate_search_identifiers may

Implementations should prefer reading the IDs via:

TargetIdentifiers are a collection of identifiers which uniquely identify the target.

All possible identifiers are stored in the catalog_identifier map, but a few interesting ones to systems

requestedIntegrationTime.description: Integration time calculated by the system for the configured entity.

actualIntegrationTime.description: Integration time spent on sky (may be different than the system calculated time).

view.description:
Which view should be presented when not querying a specific value.

Business rules determine which value to show the user.
Clients should use this field to display requested or actual times when both cannot be displayed.
Contact the CFHT QSO Team for more information.

validatedTimeOnSky.description: The time spent observing validated exposures (including repeats) for the entity.

timeOnSky.description: The time spent observing *ALL* exposures (repeats, validated, or not validated) for the entity.

persistenceTimeAccountingData.description:
Specific parameters for tracking observatory time usage by a scientific program.

Each parameter is an object that belongs to an entity which describes a series of exposures.
